landing: Good ratings cluster on one thing — speed and helpfulness. The praise is consistent (fast, prompt, genuinely helpful replies) and spans onboarding, account access, aliases, and appointments. The support experience itself is landing well across the board when the team responds quickly.
not_landing: The handful of bad ratings are about product gaps, not support quality — and one has already flipped positive. A couple are misdirected (wrong-product or confused users, language barriers — not real Thundermail dissatisfaction). The genuine technical ones still open are an aliases delivery bug and a data-deletion/privacy follow-up. The custom-domain DKIM/deliverability issue turned around after engineering followed up and shipped a fix — effectively a positive now (kudos to eng). Pricing also surfaces even inside positive ratings — one happy customer still flagged it as a future risk.
